title: Engineering Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to the application of physical laws and principles of engineering for the development of machines, materials, instruments, processes, and services. Includes teachers of subjects such as chemical, civil, electrical, industrial, mechanical, mineral, and petroleum engineering. Includes both teachers primarily engaged in teaching and those who do a combination of teaching and research.

tasks.text: Conduct research in a particular field of knowledge and publish findings in professional journals, books, or electronic media.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare course materials, such as syllabi, homework assignments, and handouts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Evaluate and grade students' class work, laboratory work, assignments, and papers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Write grant proposals to procure external research funding.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise undergraduate or graduate teaching, internship, and research work.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Keep abreast of developments in the field by reading current literature, talking with colleagues, and participating in professional conferences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare and deliver lectures to undergraduate or graduate students on topics such as mechanics, hydraulics, and robotics.
